meta.subject: RE-UE4SS timeline focused on the TFWWorkbench C++ ABI break on The Forever Winter (UE5.4.2)
meta.versioning: git-describe 'v3.0.1-<commits-since-tag>-g<hash>'. NO stable release since v3.0.1; the only 'latest' is the rolling experimental-latest prerelease (the unreleased v4.0.0-rc1 line).
meta.notes: breaking=true flags a change relevant to a precompiled C++ mod (ABI) or to a runtime DataTable editor. The actual TFWWorkbench breaker is C++ ABI drift enabled by the xmake->CMake migration, NOT the FName flip (red herring).

versions.note: Final stable release; the anchor tag for all git-describe experimental version strings.
Covers TFW's UE5.4.2. Additive. Rules out an engine-version-support gap.
Files move to <game>/ue4ss/; proxy DLL stays in Win64. Adds [Overrides] ModsFolderPaths/ControllingModsTxt (present on the local machine). Backwards-compatible; not the breaker.
Single rolling prerelease; assets force-updated continuously since. Why 'latest' is a moving target with no version bump.

BREAKING C++ change but a RED HERRING for TFWWorkbench (mod passes FNAME_Add explicitly; default not part of the mangled symbol). Verification refuted the sub-claim that it changed the Lua FName() default.
Changelog: 'cannot guarantee ABI compatability.' The root ENABLER of the C++ mod ABI instability that later bit TFWWorkbench.

versions.version: TObjectPtr<> reworked as real smart pointer (PR #850)
versions.note: BREAKING (v4.0.0-rc line; merge date unconfirmed). On the surface the mod touches via UDataTable::GetRowStruct() -> TObjectPtr<UScriptStruct>&. Candidate for a load-succeeds-then-crashes failure mode.
Commits 91b70e5b8b1f (=848) / 486806ab2bed (=849). Nexus floor 'v3.0.1-848 or higher.' Inferred ABI baseline for TFWWorkbench v0.2.1's main.dll.
main is 1011 commits ahead of the v3.0.1 tag. ~160 commits of C++ ABI drift past the mod's Jan-2026 build. Breaks TFWWorkbench v0.2.1 (0x7F).
